name: Saxon Math
description: Decades-proven spiral math curriculum with incremental lessons and daily review for strong retention and high test scores
notes: Gold standard for spiral/procedural math. Constant review builds retention but can feel tedious for quick learners. Dry black-and-white presentation. Lessons are 45-60 min in upper grades. Homeschool kits include textbook, tests, worksheets, and solutions manual. Manipulatives kit ($75-180) required for K-3. Some consider it the reason they love math; others the reason they hate it.

name: Singapore Math (Dimensions)
description: Rigorous mastery-based curriculum using the Concrete-Pictorial-Abstract approach that produces top global math performers
notes: Based on Singapore's world-leading math methodology. Dimensions Math is the newer flagship series (PK-8); Primary Mathematics is the classic (K-6) with multiple editions (US, Standards, 2022). Strong focus on bar modeling, mental math, and conceptual depth. Best for math-confident parents; less-confident parents may want video supplements. Grades 6-8 have a noticeably different presentation than K-5.

name: Math-U-See
description: Multisensory mastery-based program with manipulative blocks and video instruction covering K through Calculus
notes: Uses colored manipulative blocks so students visualize math concepts before working abstractly. Video instruction by Steve Demme teaches each lesson. 13 levels (Primer through Calculus) are mastery-based, not grade-based — students advance when ready. Best for visual/kinesthetic learners and students who struggled with other programs. Some families find the slower mastery pace frustrating; blocks don't help every learner equally.

name: Beast Academy
description: Challenging comic-book-style math curriculum focused on problem-solving and advanced mathematical thinking
notes: Created by the Art of Problem Solving team. Comic-book-style guides with monster characters teach advanced concepts through puzzles and logical reasoning. Online platform ($16/mo or $100/yr) or print books ($27 per guide+practice set). The most challenging elementary math program available. NOT for struggling math students — designed for kids who love math and are easily bored by drills. Levels don't align with traditional grades; start 1-2 levels below current grade. After Beast Academy, students continue with AoPS for middle/high school.

name: RightStart Mathematics
description: Hands-on abacus-based curriculum using games and visualization instead of rote drills, developed by a Montessori educator
notes: Developed by Dr. Joan Cotter, a Montessori educator. Uses the specially designed AL Abacus as its primary tool. 300+ card games replace flash card drills. Scripted lessons are open-and-go but require one-on-one parent instruction (30-45 min daily). Levels A-H (K-8). Initial manipulatives investment ~$218 (Math Set) is a one-time purchase. Excellent for children with learning challenges like dyscalculia. Primarily suited for individual instruction, not group settings.

name: Teaching Textbooks
description: Self-teaching app-based math program with video lessons and automatic grading — the most hands-off option for parents
notes: The most-recommended 'easy to implement' math curriculum. App-based with video lessons, automatic grading, and hints for wrong answers. Spiral approach. Free trial (first 15 lessons). Family plan capped at $199/year for unlimited kids. Works on any device, even offline (up to 6 lessons). Daily parent email progress updates. Moves at a gentler pace than some programs — may need to go one level above grade. College-prep but not as rigorous as Singapore or Beast Academy. Pre-Calculus is the highest level offered.

name: Math Mammoth
description: Affordable mastery-based worktext emphasizing conceptual understanding and self-directed learning with minimal prep
notes: Light Blue Series is the complete curriculum (grades 1-7); Blue Series is topical/supplemental. Written directly to the student for self-teaching. PDF format is printable for siblings at no extra cost — entire K-7 curriculum for ~$150-275 total. Meets/exceeds Common Core Standards. Author Maria Miller excels at simplifying complex math concepts. No built-in hands-on activities or teacher scripts. Best for independent learners with math-comfortable parents. After grade 7, students move to AoPS or traditional Algebra 1 from another publisher.

name: Life of Fred
description: Story-based math series following a child prodigy's hilarious adventures, teaching real-world math through narrative
notes: Students follow Fred Gauss, a 5-year-old math professor at KITTENS University. Hardcover, non-consumable, reusable books (~$200 for 10 elementary books). Created by Dr. Stanley Schmidt. Faith-neutral but includes occasional mentions of God/church — not fully secular. Light on practice problems; supplementing with 'Zillions of Practice Problems' books is recommended. Best as a supplement or for self-motivated readers who hate traditional math. Elementary series has only 38-57 lessons/year, which may not be sufficient as a standalone. Through high school and into college-level topics (linear algebra, statistics).

name: The Good and the Beautiful Math
description: Affordable spiral math with beautiful design and a gentle Christian worldview — much of the digital curriculum is free
notes: Math K-7 digital downloads are free; printed materials are $10-40. General Christian worldview (gratitude, honesty, prayer) without denominational doctrine — produced by members of the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints. Spiral approach revisits concepts regularly. Beautifully designed, colorful materials. Curriculum runs advanced; take the placement test as many students place below their grade. Some lessons are more hands-on than expected. Not suitable for secular families without significant tweaking.

name: Horizons Math
description: Colorful, fast-paced spiral curriculum with hands-on activities and teacher-directed lessons from a Christian publisher
notes: Brightly illustrated consumable workbooks with full-color student pages. Scope and sequence is intentionally advanced — covers concepts earlier than Common Core requires. Teacher handbook outlines every step of each lesson. Heavy use of manipulatives in early grades (dominoes, counters, play money, abacus). Requires one-on-one teacher instruction. Covers K-6 plus a Pre-Algebra course. Complete sets run ~$120/grade. Some parents report concepts being skipped or poorly explained in upper grades.

name: ShillerMath
description: Montessori-based multisensory math with scripted lessons, physical manipulatives, and zero lesson prep required
notes: Three kits cover PK-3 (Kit I, $300-400), 4th-Pre-Algebra (Kit II, $300), and Fractions ($125). Each kit spans 4-5 years so per-year cost is reasonable. Includes 12+ physical manipulatives (balance, number tiles, wooden shapes, decimal cubes, etc.). Completely scripted — just read what's in quotes. 15 min/day for age 4, 30-45 min for age 9+. Lifetime downloads for consumable lessons. Virtual-only options available at $80-125/kit. Limited online reviews. Some parents report retention concerns and inconsistencies in teaching guides.
